title: Harvester settlement / redeem / learning-write separation
intent: Harvester run_harvester() splits its three side-effect responsibilities into three callable functions with three independent authority gates: (1) record_settlement_result(...) writes the settlement fact only, (2) enqueue_redeem_command(...) is a separate lifecycle transition that uses the existing SettlementState.REDEEM_INTENT_CREATED state machine in src/execution/settlement_commands.py, and (3) maybe_write_learning_pair(...) refuses to call harvest_settlement(...) without explicit source/lineage authority — settlement-recorded is no longer a synonym for redeem-confirmed nor for training-data-eligible.

asserted_invariants.text: After T1C, run_harvester()'s settlement-recorded branch (src/execution/harvester.py:638 _settle_positions and :655-657 store_settlement_records) does not invoke any redeem-state transition. Redeem transitions only occur through the SettlementState.REDEEM_INTENT_CREATED → REDEEM_SUBMITTED → REDEEM_CONFIRMED chain in src/execution/settlement_commands.py:71-77, called from a separate enqueue_redeem_command(...) function with its own preconditions.

harvest_settlement(...) (src/execution/harvester.py:1569) is unreachable from run_harvester() unless the calling context provides a non-empty source_model_version AND snapshot_training_allowed=True (or its equivalent post-T1C lineage object). The existing reject-on-missing-issue-time guard at lines 1597-1603 is preserved; T1C ADDS a hard rejection on missing source/lineage authority and emits harvester_learning_write_blocked_total{reason} on each block.

When a snapshot's authority is anything other than the explicit training-source allowlist (per _is_training_forecast_source at src/execution/harvester.py:181), harvest_settlement(...) returns 0 pairs and increments harvester_learning_write_blocked_total{reason='live_praw_no_training_lineage'}. Test test_harvester_does_not_rebrand_live_praw_as_training_without_lineage asserts this on a synthetic Open-Meteo / live snapshot input.

ZEUS_HARVESTER_LIVE_ENABLED default OFF gate at src/execution/harvester.py:461, src/execution/harvester_pnl_resolver.py:44, and src/ingest/harvester_truth_writer.py:443 remains unchanged. T1C DOES NOT add any new env var that enables harvester live.

T1C does NOT modify src/state/lifecycle_manager.py. If T1C scope analysis reveals a lifecycle-manager change is required, executor stops and re-plans (this is K0/K1 surface and is forbidden by phase-local scope.yaml).

round2_grep_verification.settlement_state_machine_already_exists: src/execution/settlement_commands.py:71-77 SettlementState.REDEEM_INTENT_CREATED/REDEEM_SUBMITTED/REDEEM_TX_HASHED/REDEEM_CONFIRMED/REDEEM_FAILED/REDEEM_RETRYING/REDEEM_REVIEW_REQUIRED — T1C uses this; does not invent new states.
round2_grep_verification.settlement_record_path: src/execution/harvester.py:638 _settle_positions(...) is the position-settlement function; src/execution/harvester.py:655-657 store_settlement_records (imported from src/state/decision_chain.py:217) is the settlement-record writer. Today these are inline in run_harvester(); T1C extracts them into named functions.
round2_grep_verification.learning_pair_path: src/execution/harvester.py:611-633 is the inline 'event_pairs += harvest_settlement(...)' loop. harvest_settlement is defined at src/execution/harvester.py:1569 and already has TWO authority guards: (a) line 1610-1612 raises ValueError if p_raw_vector is set without source_model_version, (b) line 1597-1603 returns 0 if forecast_issue_time is missing. T1C ADDS a third hard guard: lineage authority check on the snapshot/source pair, with the new counter.
round2_grep_verification.no_existing_counter_sink: Planner grep (src/state/, src/observability/, src/riskguard/metrics.py) found no general counter sink. The three *_observation modules in src/state/ are read-only projections, not increment surfaces. T1BD is the first phase to introduce the counter sink decision; T1C reuses whatever T1BD chose.

t1c_known_gaps_coverage_per_T-1_KNOWN_GA.G-HARV1: Harvester live settlement write is HIGH-only for LOW markets — T1C covers via the metric_identity logic at harvester.py:1614-1618; the fix landed in tests/test_harvester_metric_identity.py (already green per repo state).
t1c_known_gaps_coverage_per_T-1_KNOWN_GA.G-HARV2: Settlement observation lookup ignores authority/station/metric — T1C covers via _lookup_settlement_obs at harvester.py:391 and _station_matches_city at :381 (both already exist; T1C only adds tests if coverage is missing).
t1c_known_gaps_coverage_per_T-1_KNOWN_GA.G-SETTLE: Settled pending-exit exposure can be skipped indefinitely — T1C covers via _settle_positions extraction; ensures settled rows write even when the caller is in pending_exit phase.
t1c_known_gaps_coverage_per_T-1_KNOWN_GA.G-HARV3: Harvester can rebrand live decision p_raw as TIGGE training data — this is the PRIMARY G-HARV3 antibody; covered by T1C-LIVE-PRAW-NOT-TRAINING-DATA invariant + new test.

_planner_notes.loc_estimate_basis: harvester.py is 1968 lines today. T1C extracts ~150 lines of inline logic from run_harvester() (lines 504-689 region) into 3 named functions, adds ~200 lines of authority-check + counter wiring, and adds ~250 lines of new test code across two test files. Total ~600 LOC delta is conservative.
_planner_notes.topology_admission: BLOCKED at planner time — generic profile does not admit src/execution/harvester.py for write-intent edit. Resolution per LOCK_DECISION Amendment 4.
_planner_notes.k0_k1_classification: K2 execution surface (harvester is execution/runtime not state-truth). NOT K0 — but adjacent because settlement_commands.py is K2 settlement_redeem_command_model. Critic should be opus per ORCHESTRATOR_RUNBOOK §12 T1C row.
coordinator_blockers: T0.2 RiskGuard still RUNNING (PID 14177); operator must unload before dispatch (planner-Round-1 amendment 1 carried forward; coordinator confirmed escalation pending).
T0.3 venue quiescent attestation pending (planner-Round-1 carried forward).
Counter-sink module not yet decided; T1BD (predecessor) makes that decision and T1C consumes it.
_planner_notes.predecessor_dependency: T1BD MUST land first because T1C reuses the counter-sink and increments harvester_learning_write_blocked_total — same sink as cost_basis_chain_mutation_blocked_total that T1BD introduces.
